svg.title: Sheva Berachos / שבע ברכות — Liturgical Typography Series
svg.desc: ArtScroll-faithful bilingual liturgical spread: bold Hebrew blessings with interlinear English gloss, folio headers, commentary columns, and cold archival paper texture.

g.text: ‹ Who has created «« everything «« of the universe, ‹ King ‹ our God, ‹ Hashem, ‹ are You, ‹ Blessed
אַתָּה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ מֶלֶךְ הָעוֹלָם, יוֹצֵר הָאָדָם.
‹ Who fashioned «« of the universe, ‹ King ‹ our God, ‹ Hashem, ‹ are You, ‹ Blessed
‹ delight, ‹ pleasure, ‹ glad song, ‹ rejoicing, ‹ and gladness, ‹ groom and bride, ‹ joy*
מְהֵרָה יְהוָה אֱלֹהֵינוּ יִשָּׁמַע בְּעָרֵי יְהוּדָה
וּבְחוּצוֹת יְרוּשָׁלַיִם קוֹל שָׂשׂוֹן וְקוֹל שִׂמְחָה,
‹ and the ‹ of joy ‹ the ‹ of Jerusalem, ‹ and in the ‹ of Judah ‹ let there be heard
«« of grooms ‹ of the jubilance «« of the ‹ the «« of gladness, ‹ voice ‹ groom ‹ bride
בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה, מְשַׂמֵּחַ חָתָן וְכַלָּה.
«« the bride. ‹ with ‹ the groom ‹ Who gladdens ‹ Hashem,
שָׂשׂוֹן — Joy … and companionship. Ten expressions of joy allude to the ten canopies
which God erected for Adam and Eve in the Garden of Eden (Bava Basra 75a).
מְשַׂמֵּחַ חָתָן וְכַלָּה — Who gladdens groom and bride, individually; for each must be
the recipient of God's blessing. The following blessing is recited from days of old.
AFTER THE RING IS PLACED ON THE BRIDE'S FINGER, THE
Kesubah (MARRIAGE CONTRACT) IS READ ALOUD. A SECOND CUP
OF WINE IS POURED AND THE SEVEN BLESSINGS (
שֶׁבַע בְּרָכוֹת) ARE RECITED ALOUD. THE HONOR OF RECITING
THESE SEVEN BLESSINGS MAY BE DIVIDED AMONG SEVERAL PEOPLE.
When Sheva Berachos are recited, the Leader recites the following Zimun,
with a cup of wine in hand.
«« [let us bless. ‹ Gentlemen,] «« bless. ‹ let us ‹ Gentlemen,
יְהִי שֵׁם יְהוָה מְבֹרָךְ מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם.
«« eternity. ‹ until ‹ from ‹ be ‹ of the ‹ Let this time blessed ‹ Hashem Name
יְהִי שֵׁם יְהוָה מְבֹרָךְ מֵעַתָּה וְעַד עוֹלָם.
«« eternity. ‹ until ‹ from ‹ be ‹ of the ‹ Let ‹ this time blessed ‹ Hashem Name
IN MANY CONGREGATIONS THE FOLLOWING IS NOT RECITED ON THE SABBATH:
‹ amidst ‹ her through the «« — the barren ‹ and exult ‹ May she rejoice
בְּשִׂמְחָה. בָּרוּךְ אַתָּה יְהוָה, מְשַׂמֵּחַ צִיּוֹן בְּבָנֶיהָ.
«« through her children.* ‹ Zion ‹ Who gladdens «« Hashem, ‹ are You, ‹ Blessed «« in gladness.
‹ Your creation, ‹ as You «« who are beloved,* ‹ the companions ‹ Gladden intensely*
The number seven in Jewish thought denotes completion — seven days
in the week, seven years in the Sabbatical Year cycle, seven days to
inaugurate the Tabernacle, etc. Both Jacob and Samson celebrated their
דּוֹנַי הֵסֵר — Pain banish. The first letters of each phrase spell
רוּנַ"ז. Donash ben Labrat, composer of this brief poem, was a 10th-century
pioneer Hebrew grammarian, frequently cited by Rashi.
